description:
Render paths (bl show/list/ready and their text renderers) write task title, description, tags, and note text raw to the TTY with zero control-character handling. The only escape-aware code (strip_ansi_len) is column-width math that deliberately passes ANSI through, confirming the pipeline assumes its content strings are trusted.

WHY THIS MATTERS NOW: that assumption holds only while every task is locally authored. Bidirectional external sync (the GitHub-issues plugin) makes title/description/tags/notes attacker-controlled for the first time. A hostile issue title carrying CSI/OSC sequences can forge what bl list/show display (hide or fake a task, spoof an id/owner — corrupting the very tool you trust for state), poison the clipboard via OSC 52 so a later paste runs an attacker command, or spoof hyperlinks via OSC 8. External content is DATA, not terminal commands.

PRINCIPLE: balls must neutralize control sequences at the display boundary, so no foreign string can ever drive the user's terminal. Sanitize at RENDER time, not ingest: the trust boundary is 'untrusted bytes -> TTY', it keeps the --json machine contract byte-exact (serde already \u-escapes; JSON consumers are unaffected), and one chokepoint covers every current and future external source.

SCOPE:
- Add a content sanitizer applied to externally-influenced fields on all human/TTY render paths (render_show, render_show_text, render_list, render_ready, progress).
- Neutralize C0 (0x00-0x1F) except tab; collapse newlines in single-line/inline contexts; neutralize C1 (0x80-0x9F), DEL (0x7F), and ESC-introduced CSI/OSC. Replace with a visible inert marker rather than silently dropping.
- Sanitize CONTENT before it is composed with balls' own SGR styling — do not blanket-strip final output (balls legitimately emits its own ANSI).
- Do NOT touch --json output (machine contract).
- Preserve legitimate Unicode (emoji, accents, CJK).
- 100% coverage including the standard payloads: bare ESC, CSI color/cursor/erase, OSC 0/8/52, CR/BS/DEL, C1; plus a golden render proving balls' own styling still works with a malicious title.

OUT OF SCOPE (separate hardening, not filed here): ingest bounds — max title length, tag count/charset, max creates-per-sync, unbounded verbatim external map.
title: Neutralize terminal control sequences in rendered task content
